name: From the monastery of Sant Miguel to La Buitrera — circular from Llíria
name_original: Del monasterio de Sant Miguel a La Buitrera — circular desde Llíria
region_intro: Hiking around Llíria offers diverse landscapes at the convergence of the mountainous foothills of the Sierra Calderona range and the agricultural plains along the Turia River. The region features lush Mediterranean forests, small mountain ranges like Les Travesses, and the Turia River valley. Elevations include peaks such as La Buitrera (293 m) and Puntal de Llop (598 m), providing varied terrain from accessible parks to more challenging mountain routes. The area is characterized by holm oaks, pine forests, and aromatic shrubs. Best hiking trails around Llíria The most popular hiking route is From the monastery of Sant Miguel to La Buitrera — circular from Llíria, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route explores low-altitude peaks and passes by the Iberian archaeological site of Edeta and the Monastery of Sant Miquel. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Buitrera loop from Llíria, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This trail offers diverse natural and ethnographic insights, reaching the peak of La Buitrera. Local hikers also love the San Vicente Park loop from Iglesia Evangelica Filadelfia, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through lush forests and past a natural water source, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around Llíria is defined by mountainous foothills, agricultural plains, and Mediterranean forests, with routes offering options for easy walks and more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Llíria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Llíria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Llíria?
A: Llíria offers a diverse range of over 30 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Llíria?
A: Yes, Llíria has several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38234170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Park loop from Iglesia Evangelica Filadelfia&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.0 miles (6.5 km) long and leads through lush forests and past a natural water source. San Vicente Park itself is known for its accessible paths, picnic areas, and playgrounds.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Llíria?
A: Absolutely. Many of Llíria's trails are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375131713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the monastery of Sant Miguel to La Buitrera — circular from Llíria&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.8-mile (7.8 km) route that explores low-altitude peaks and historical sites.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Llíria?
A: Llíria's landscape is incredibly varied, situated where the mountainous foothills of the Sierra Calderona meet the agricultural plains of the Turia River. You'll encounter lush Mediterranean forests with carob, olive, pine, and cypress trees, small mountain ranges like Les Travesses, and areas rich in biodiversity with holm oaks and aromatic shrubs. Some trails also offer views of rocky areas and canyons near the Turia River.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Llíria?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Llíria offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318002432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Cavalló de l'Olivera (PR-CV 258) — circular desde Casa del Camp&lt;/a&gt;. This is a difficult 13.5-mile (21.8 km) circular route that requires good fitness and takes you through more demanding terrain.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the trails in Llíria?
A: Hikers can discover several natural attractions and viewpoints. The small Les Travesses mountain range offers peaks like La Buitrera (293 m) with scenic views. For a more expansive vista, Puntal de Llop (598 m) provides an authentic natural viewpoint overlooking the Turia River valley. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4579981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Frailecico Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/889460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mireia Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, Llíria's trails often weave through areas of historical significance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375131713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the monastery of Sant Miguel to La Buitrera — circular from Llíria&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the Iberian archaeological site of Edeta and the Monastery of Sant Miquel. Another notable site is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2725439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Puntal dels Llops — Iberian archaeological site&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Llíria?
A: The hiking trails in Llíria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from accessible parks to challenging mountain routes, and the blend of natural beauty with historical and cultural elements.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Llíria?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, Llíria is generally well-equipped for visitors. Many popular starting points, especially those closer to the town or within parks like San Vicente, typically offer designated parking areas or street parking nearby. It's advisable to check specific route information for the most accurate parking guidance.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Llíria?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Llíria makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for hiking, with pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Winter can also be suitable for hiking, offering cooler weather. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during this season.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to water features or wild swimming spots?
A: While Llíria is not directly known for wild swimming spots on its hiking trails, San Vicente Park features a significant natural water source. The broader region, including areas near the Turia River, does have rocky areas and canyons. For specific water features, you might explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Azul of Chulilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is a bit further afield but a popular natural lake.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Llíria using public transport?
A: Llíria is connected by public transport, including bus services that can take you to the town center. From there, some trails, particularly those within or starting near the urban area like the San Vicente Park loops, are easily accessible on foot. For trails further out in areas like La Concordia, you might need to combine public transport with a short taxi ride or plan for a longer walk to the trailhead.
